cards.f: Relaunched Mahesh Babu as a mass hero. Remade in four languages. Bhumika Chawla became a star overnight.
cards.c: "Village rowdy falls for a city girl. Her family is horrified. He defeats everyone in his path. Then does it again."

cards.f: Highest-grossing Telugu film of its time. Remade as Wanted in Hindi. Puri Jagannadh wrote and directed.
cards.c: "Undercover cop infiltrates a gang. Falls for the villain's sister. Has to arrest the family he has come to love."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu + Trivikram dialogue = instant box office record. The one-liners became memes within hours.
cards.c: "Cop's estranged son meets his father by chance. Together they catch the villain the father never could. Then feelings happen."

cards.f: Trivikram Srinivas wrote this. Considered by many critics to be Mahesh Babu's finest performance.
cards.c: "Trained assassin takes on the identity of a harmless villager. Has to be gentle. Does not know how. Falls in love anyway."

cards.f: Shot over five years. Highest-grossing Indian film of its time. The waterfall was entirely CGI.
cards.c: "Foundling raised by a waterfall tribe discovers he might be royalty. Climbs the waterfall to confirm. Correct."

cards.f: The answer to #KattapaKyunMara broke the Indian internet. The film made Rs 1800 crore worldwide.
cards.c: "The question everyone asked for two years is finally answered. The answer raises more questions."

cards.f: Shahid Kapoor remade this as Kabir Singh. Both films broke records. Both films started arguments.
cards.c: "Brilliant surgeon self-destructs over a breakup. Everyone watches. Nobody stops him. He is very good looking so this takes longer than it should."

cards.f: S.S. Rajamouli. The Naatu Naatu dance sequence won the Oscar. It took three months to choreograph.
cards.c: "Two freedom fighters meet, become best friends, and discover they are on opposite sides. They dance about it."

cards.f: First Telugu film to use motion capture. The horse sequences were entirely practical. The horse was very brave.
cards.c: "Warrior dies 400 years ago mid-love story. Reincarnates as a stuntman. The love story is still unfinished. He notices."

cards.f: Allu Arjun spent 6 months on the accent and mannerisms. The hand tilt became a national gesture.
cards.c: "Illegitimate son of a landlord becomes the most powerful red sandalwood smuggler in Andhra. Tilts his hand. Becomes a meme."

cards.f: Won the National Award for Best Telugu Film. Made on a tiny budget. The food truck was real and operational.
cards.c: "Two people meet for an arranged marriage meeting. Both are trying to escape their families' plans. They accidentally start a food truck."

cards.f: Shot largely in and around Warangal. Sai Pallavi did not have a body double for a single scene.
cards.c: "NRI visits Telangana to find a bride. Finds one immediately. She is too much for him. He leaves. He comes back."

cards.f: Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna. The bus scene alone generated 40 million YouTube views.
cards.c: "Man accidentally compromises a girl's reputation on a bus. Spends the rest of the film apologising. She does not accept easily."

cards.f: Allu Arjun starrer. Butta Bomma became the most streamed Telugu song of the year. Babies swapped is the oldest plot. Still works.
cards.c: "Babies swapped at birth. One grows up rich, one grows up poor. Rich father eventually notices something is off."

cards.f: Siddharth's career-defining role. The father-son dynamic was so accurately written that fathers complained.
cards.c: "Overprotected son rebels against controlling father. Falls for a free-spirited girl. Father is the actual obstacle. Father eventually gets it."

cards.f: Sekhar Kammula's most loved film. No songs during the film. No villain. Just people. Telugu audiences were disoriented then devoted.
cards.c: "Group of engineering students navigate college. No villain. No item numbers. Just life. Telugu audiences were confused then loved it."

cards.f: Highest-grossing Telugu film of 2013. Pawan Kalyan's biggest commercial hit. Trivikram Srinivas dialogue.
cards.c: "Grandson goes undercover in his estranged grandfather's house to reunite the family. Grandfather has no idea. Very large house."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu starrer. The adoption of a village as a CSR concept was modelled on real government schemes.
cards.c: "Billionaire's son adopts a poor village to prove himself. Village has problems. He solves them. Village is grateful. Father is proud."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu played a Chief Minister who uses an app to receive public complaints. The app trended for a week.
cards.c: "Young man inherits the Chief Minister position. Has no political experience. Governs better than everyone before him. Politicians are annoyed."

cards.f: Jr NTR's transformation from corrupt to principled cop was the most dramatic character arc of that year.
cards.c: "Corrupt cop is forced to confront his worst self after meeting a rape survivor. Becomes a good cop. The transformation is violent."

cards.f: Pawan Kalyan and Bhoomika Chawla. The hate-to-love formula executed at full Telugu mass-cinema volume.
cards.c: "Two people hate each other from the moment they meet. Stuck together by circumstance. Hate becomes something else. Surprise."

cards.f: Allu Arjun and Trivikram. The comedy sequences between Allu Arjun and Brahmanandam ran longer than the plot.
cards.c: "Lazy man accidentally gets recruited by both a con man and the cops. Plays both sides. Is surprisingly good at this."

cards.f: Panja Vaisshnav Tej's debut. The climax on the sea was shot in real waves. Nobody was insured for this.
cards.c: "Fisherman's son falls for a landlord's daughter. Landlord disagrees with his whole body. The sea is involved in the ending."

cards.f: Naveen Polishetty and Nazriya Fahadh. The lie they construct across two families escalates geometrically.
cards.c: "Hindu boy and Christian girl want to marry. Both families need elaborate convincing. They construct an increasingly complicated lie."

cards.f: Nani's career-best performance. Based on a true story. Shahid Kapoor remade it in Hindi. Neither version is easy to finish without crying.
cards.c: "Washed-up cricketer attempts a comeback at 36 to buy his son a jersey. Son does not need the jersey. Dad needs this."

cards.f: Venkatesh and Bhoomika. One of the first Telugu supernatural comedies. Spawned an entire subgenre.
cards.c: "A ghost is in love with the hero. He is already engaged. She does not care. Very, very persistent ghost."

cards.f: Charmi's career-defining performance. The thriller structure was unprecedented in mainstream Telugu cinema at the time.
cards.c: "Woman wakes up in a stranger's house with no memory of the previous night. Retraces her steps. Each step is worse than the last."

cards.f: Ravi Teja in a double role. The real cop and the real villager had to be equally convincing. Only one succeeds.
cards.c: "Fierce cop is mistaken for a meek villager and vice versa. Cop has to be meek. Villager has to be fierce. Neither is suited to this."

cards.f: Ravi Teja starrer. The adrenaline addiction concept was written before it was clinically defined in Telugu mass cinema.
cards.c: "Man is addicted to the feeling of doing something for the first time. This is a quirky trait until it becomes a superpower."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu starrer. The village deity angle was taken completely seriously. The auto-driver did not want this responsibility.
cards.c: "Auto driver is prophesied to be a god by a village. He does not want to be a god. The village insists. He reluctantly becomes a god."

cards.f: Rana Daggubati's debut. The political idealism felt naïve and then felt necessary. Both at the same time.
cards.c: "Young man accidentally becomes a politician. Turns out to be an honest one. The entire system tries to destroy him for this."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu and Venkatesh together. The family drama ran longer than needed and nobody complained.
cards.c: "Two brothers love the same woman. One steps aside. The other doesn't know this for years. When he finds out, the film earns its runtime."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu starrer. Sukumar directed. Visually the most ambitious Telugu film of that decade.
cards.c: "Rock star cannot tell what is real and what is a hallucination. Neither can the audience. This is intentional."

cards.f: Nikhil Siddhartha and Swathi. Low-budget heist comedy that punched far above its weight.
cards.c: "Small-time thief and a woman on the run are thrown together. Both are lying about everything. They fall in love anyway."

cards.f: Prabhas before Baahubali. The flashback romance is considered one of the finest in Telugu cinema.
cards.c: "Man visits his ancestral village and inherits a decades-old enemy along with the property. Enemy was not listed in the will."

cards.f: Allu Arjun starrer. Trivikram wrote the father character as a specific critique of unchecked generosity.
cards.c: "Son of a generous man inherits his father's generosity and also his father's debts. Must repay both. Somehow does."

cards.f: Remake of the Malayalam original. Vijay Deverakonda in a breakout supporting role. Three love stories, one man.
cards.c: "Man falls in love three times across different decades of his life. Each time he is a different person. Third time it works."

cards.f: Allu Arjun played a brahmin priest with absolute commitment. The dual identity gag sustained an entire film.
cards.c: "Devout brahmin priest is secretly a contract killer. Has to keep these two identities separate. At a wedding. Challenging."

cards.f: Mahesh Babu starrer. The NRI-returns-and-fixes-farming plot was earnest in a way Telugu blockbusters rarely allow.
cards.c: "Successful NRI returns to his village after guilt catches up with him. Turns the village into a model farm. Guilt partially resolved."

cards.f: Suhas and Chandini Chowdary. The 1990s period detail was meticulously researched. The ending does not comfort.
cards.c: "Lower-caste boy falls for upper-caste girl in 1990s Andhra. His caste is discovered. The film does not look away from what happens next."

cards.f: Pawan Kalyan and Rana Daggubati. Remake of Ayyappanum Koshiyum. Two men and their ego. That's the film.
cards.c: "Two men of equal stubbornness clash over ego and principle. Nobody backs down. The state suffers. The audience cheers."

cards.f: Nagarjuna's biggest commercial hit. The anti-marriage argument is presented so convincingly that the reversal has to work harder.
cards.c: "Committed bachelor with a mission to stay single meets someone who dismantles every argument he has. He argues until he can't."

cards.f: Siddharth and Trisha. The impossible task structure goes through three iterations. Each one escalates.
cards.c: "Man loves a girl whose family demands an impossible task. He does it. They add another task. He does that too."

cards.f: Prabhas before the mega-stardom. The rowdy-with-a-heart role that built the template.
cards.c: "Small-time rowdy with a big heart navigates loyalty, love, and violence in equal measure. Survives mostly on instinct."

cards.f: Venkatesh starrer. The criminal family versus romance tension was handled with more nuance than the genre usually allows.
cards.c: "Man raised in a criminal family tries to go straight for the woman he loves. Family has other plans. Violence has other plans."

cards.f: Allu Arjun and Sheela. The girl's rebellion is treated with more seriousness than the genre usually affords.
cards.c: "Girl runs away from home repeatedly. A man keeps being sent to bring her back. He does. She runs again. He starts to understand why."

cards.f: Sri Simha and Satya. The crime comedy subgenre in Telugu needed exactly this film.
cards.c: "Two broke men accidentally stumble into a murder investigation while trying to recover their stolen money. They are not detectives. It shows."

cards.f: Venkatesh and Nayanthara. The rom-com formula executed at senior star level. The chemistry was real.
cards.c: "Easygoing man falls for a fierce woman. She does not want to fall for him. She falls anyway. He does not notice immediately."

cards.f: Three generations of the Akkineni family acting together. Akkineni Nageswara Rao's final film. The meta dimension is overwhelming.
cards.c: "Three generations of one family are reincarnations of each other. They all meet. The family reunion is complicated."

cards.f: Ravi Teja and Richa Gangopadhyay. The hired-troublemaker-falls-in-love plot runs on pure mass energy.
cards.c: "Rowdy is hired to cause problems in a village. Falls for a girl there. Has to stop causing problems. His employer is unhappy."

cards.f: Pawan Kalyan starrer. The family history reveal runs through two decades of backstory in one interval.
cards.c: "Man discovers his girlfriend's family has a complicated history with his family. History is putting it mildly."

cards.f: Sharwanand starrer. The grandfather-grandson relationship became the benchmark for sentiment in Telugu commercial cinema.
cards.c: "Grandfather and grandson share a bond that skips a generation. Grandfather's final wish launches the grandson's life. Quietly devastating."

cards.f: Allu Arjun's mass breakthrough. Puri Jagannadh direction. The action sequences were the most expensive in Telugu cinema at that point.
cards.c: "Journalist investigates a crime in a small town. Town does not want to be investigated. He investigates harder."

cards.f: NTR Jr and Kajal Aggarwal. Trivikram's dialogue made every line quotable. The hero pretends to be a boyfriend and the pretence becomes real.
cards.c: "Man agrees to pretend to be someone's boyfriend to fend off a villain. Meets the real family. Starts feeling things he was not contracted for."

cards.f: Nithiin and Keerthy Suresh. The childhood-enemies-to-lovers arc runs on pure physical comedy.
cards.c: "Girl has hated a specific boy since childhood. He moves next door as an adult. She still hates him. He finds this encouraging."

cards.f: K Balachander directed. Made Kamal Haasan a star in Telugu. The ending destroyed every audience that saw it.
cards.c: "Young couple elope because their families say no. They run as far as they can. They do not make it."

cards.f: Priyadarshi and Sree Vishnu. Made on a small budget. Kickstarted a wave of low-budget Telugu comedies.
cards.c: "Four friends try to cheat in their exams. One has a secret. Every plan fails more spectacularly than the last."

cards.f: K Vishwanath directed. Won the National Award. The classical music sequences are performed live, not playback.
cards.c: "Classical musician takes in a woman shunned by society. Teaches her everything. Society disapproves of this too."

cards.f: Kamal Haasan lost 20 kg for this role. The classical dance sequences are considered the finest in Telugu cinema.
cards.c: "Brilliant classical dancer destroys himself with alcohol. His student surpasses him. He watches this happen from a distance."

cards.f: Kamal Haasan won the National Award. The film is still studied in psychology courses as a depiction of intellectual disability.
cards.c: "Intellectually disabled man lives with absolute purity and kindness. The world is not designed for him. He is the best person in it."

cards.f: Ram Gopal Varma's directorial debut. Nagarjuna. The chain fight became a reference point for action choreography.
cards.c: "Student takes on a criminal network that controls his college. This is treated as a reasonable response to ragging."

cards.f: Ram Gopal Varma direction. Sridevi and Venkatesh. The on-the-run comedy thriller was unprecedented in Telugu.
cards.c: "Woman accidentally witnesses a crime. Man accidentally gets blamed for a different crime. They run together. Neither trusts the other."

cards.f: Mani Ratnam produced. Jagapathi Babu's defining role. The political violence is unflinching.
cards.c: "Young man gets drawn into political violence without fully understanding what he is entering. By the time he understands, it is too late."

cards.f: Ram Gopal Varma direction. Nagarjuna. The whodunit structure was the most complex Telugu thriller of its decade.
cards.c: "A murder. A detective. A web of people with motive. The solution is not satisfying because reality rarely is."

cards.f: Pawan Kalyan's breakthrough. The persistence-as-romance framing aged differently than it was intended.
cards.c: "Boy loves girl. Pursues her for years. She says no repeatedly. He waits. The film thinks this is romantic."

cards.f: Chiranjeevi starrer. The corruption-fighting narrative ran three hours and felt necessary for every minute.
cards.c: "Principled man takes on a corrupt system single-handedly. The system is large. He is persistent. One of them has to give."

cards.f: Prabhas and SS Rajamouli. The mother-son separation premise is executed at maximum melodrama. It earns it.
cards.c: "Mother and son separated during a boat accident. Son grows up on the wrong side of the border. Mother waits. Son fights his way back."

cards.f: JD Chakravarthy starrer. The spirit-seeking-justice subgenre begins here in Telugu cinema.
cards.c: "Woman is murdered. Her spirit cannot rest until justice is served. A man she trusts must serve it. He is reluctant."

cards.f: Nagarjuna starrer. The disguise-based wooing strategy ran through multiple cities. She kept saying no. The film thought this was funny.
cards.c: "Man woos a woman across several cities using various disguises. She keeps rejecting him. He keeps changing the disguise."

cards.f: Anushka Shetty played a queen raised as a prince. The historical accuracy was contested. The performance was not.
cards.c: "Queen raised as a prince must rule a kingdom while hiding her gender. The kingdom has opinions. She ignores them."

cards.f: Adivi Sesh and Regina Cassandra. The interrogation-room structure holds for the entire runtime. The twist recontextualises everything.
cards.c: "A man is found dead. A woman confesses. A cop interrogates her. Every version of the story changes what actually happened."

cards.f: Kalyan Ram's career-best. The ancient king in a modern hospital was played entirely straight. No winking at the audience.
cards.c: "Ancient king is transported to the present day. Discovers hospitals, democracy, and that his dynasty ended badly. Tries to go back and fix it."

cards.f: Rana Daggubati and Sai Pallavi. The Naxalite movement backdrop is the most politically serious Telugu film in a decade.
cards.c: "Woman falls in love with a Naxalite through his poetry. Joins the movement to find him. The movement is not what she imagined."

cards.f: Nani's most ambitious film. The coal mine landscape is a character. The violence is earned not gratuitous.
cards.c: "Coal mine town. Two friends on opposite sides of a power struggle. The landscape is as brutal as the men who live in it."

cards.f: Nani starrer. The auto-driver character became one of his most loved roles. Trinadha Rao Nakkina directed.
cards.c: "Auto driver falls for a girl whose father runs the local college. Father disapproves with his entire body. The auto becomes a getaway vehicle."
